info.name: Tokens & Sessions
info.description:
Lab 6 — Managing Tokens and Sessions.

**Before running:**
1. Select the **Tokens & Sessions** environment
2. Fill in `realm` (and `client_id` if not using `oidc-playground`)
3. Run **1 — Get Token (regular)** to populate `access_token` and `refresh_token`

**Request order for each task:**
- Task 1: run request 1, inspect the response body
- Task 2: run 1 → wait 60 s → run 3 → run 4
- Task 3: run 1 after changing session settings, compare `refresh_expires_in`
- Task 4: run 2, then 5
- Task 5: run 1, then 6, then revoke in admin console, then 6 again

item.name: 1 — Get Token (regular)
item.description:
Password grant (ROPC) — exchanges alice's credentials for an access token and a regular refresh token.

Stores `access_token` and `refresh_token` as collection variables.

**Requirement:** Direct Access Grants must be ON for `{{client_id}}` in Keycloak (Clients → {client} → Settings → Capability config).

item.name: 2 — Get Token (offline)
item.description:
Password grant with `offline_access` scope — returns an offline refresh token.

Key differences in the response vs request 1:
- `refresh_expires_in: 0` (offline tokens do not expire based on session idle)
- `scope` includes `offline_access`
- The refresh token JWT payload contains `"typ": "Offline"`

Stores the offline refresh token as `offline_refresh_token`.

**Requirement:** `offline_access` must be an assigned scope on `{{client_id}}`.

item.name: 3 — Refresh (valid)
item.description:
Exchanges the current `refresh_token` for a new access token and a new refresh token (rotation).

Before sending, the current `refresh_token` is saved as `old_refresh_token` — use request 4 to demonstrate that replaying it fails.

exec: // Save current refresh_token before rotation so request 4 can replay it
console.log("tokens rotated — new access_token and refresh_token stored");

item.name: 4 — Refresh (replay — expect 400)
item.description:
Attempts to reuse `old_refresh_token` (the token that was already consumed by request 3).

Expected response: `400 Bad Request` with `{"error": "invalid_grant"}`.

This demonstrates refresh token rotation: each token can only be used once. Run request 3 first.

exec: console.log("Rotation confirmed — replayed token rejected:", json.error_description);

item.name: 5 — Refresh offline token
item.description:
Exchanges the `offline_refresh_token` (set by request 2) for a new access token.

Unlike a regular refresh token, this succeeds even if the SSO session has since expired. The offline refresh token is rotated — a new one is issued and stored.

Run request 2 first to populate `offline_refresh_token`.

item.name: 7 — Logout (revoke regular session)
item.description:
RP-initiated logout — revokes the regular SSO session by passing the `refresh_token` to the OIDC logout endpoint.

Returns `204 No Content` on success. Only the session associated with the provided `refresh_token` is revoked — the offline session (stored in `offline_refresh_token`) is a separate server-side session and is NOT affected.

After running this request:
- Request 3 (Refresh valid) → `invalid_grant`
- Request 5 (Refresh offline token) → `200 OK`

> **Contrast with admin 'Log out all sessions':** that action terminates ALL sessions including offline ones. This endpoint is session-specific.

exec: console.log("Regular session revoked. offline_refresh_token is unaffected.");
console.log("Then: run '5 — Refresh offline token' → expect 200 OK");

item.name: 6 — Decode access token
item.description:
Decodes the `access_token` JWT and logs its header and payload to the Postman Console.

This is a no-op HTTP request (GET to the OIDC discovery endpoint) — all the work is done in the test script, which base64-decodes the stored `access_token` without needing to send it anywhere.

Open **View → Show Postman Console** to see the decoded claims.

> **Why not use the introspection endpoint?** Token introspection requires client authentication (`client_id` + `client_secret`). Public clients have no secret and cannot authenticate to that endpoint.

exec: // Decode the stored access token JWT without sending it to any endpoint
if (!token) { console.error("access_token is empty — run request 1 first"); return; }
